title: hiPSC Derived Neural Stem Cells Generation and Quality Control
author: Valeria Fernandez Vallone

steps.title: Day -2 or -3 Monitor morphology...
steps.instruction: Day -2 or -3 Monitor morphology and confluence (~70%) of human induced pluripotent stem cells (hiPSCs) prior starting. Perform a single cell passage using TrypLE Select or Accutase . Seed hiPSCs at a density of 0.5 x 10 5 /cm 2 onto Geltrex coated wells of a 6 well-plate in E8 medium containing ROCK inhibitor ( 10 micromolar (µM) ).

steps.title: Day -2 to -1 Perform daily...
steps.instruction: Day -2 to -1 Perform daily medium changes with 2-3 mL E8 medium without ROCK inhibitor and culture for 48:00:00 .

steps.title: Table 1
steps.instruction: Table 1. Preparation of Neural Induction Medium (NIM) %s %s

steps.title: Day 0 When cultures reach 60-80%...
steps.instruction: Day 0 When cultures reach 60-80% confluence, replace medium with Neural Induction Medium (NIM) (Table 1). Use 2 mL for one well of a 6 well-plate.

steps.title: Day 1-3 Perform daily medium change (2
steps.instruction: Day 1-3 Perform daily medium change (2.5 – 3.5 mL / well) with NIM.

steps.title: It is possible to perform one...
steps.instruction: It is possible to perform one double feed (8 mL/ well), with the next medium change 48 h later. However, it is not recommended either to go longer than 1 day without a medium change, or to feed the cultures every other day continuously. Due to high cell density in the culture from day 4 onwards, doubling the volume of NIM is very crucial for cell nutrition. Also, minimal cell death should be observed from day 4 to 7 after neural induction. If the color of cells turns brownish with many floating cells during day 4 to 7 of neural induction, it indicates that the starting density of iPSCs was too high and should be reduced next time.

steps.title: REAGENTS PREPARATION Dorsomorphin (BMP inhibitor) Prepare...
steps.instruction: REAGENTS PREPARATION Dorsomorphin (BMP inhibitor) Prepare a 10 millimolar (mM) stock solution in DMSO, therefore dissolve 5 mg Dorsomorphin in 1.25 mL of DMSO. Make aliquots á 50 µL and store at -20 °C . Once thawed, keep at 4 °C . SB431542 (TGF-β inhibitor) Prepare a 50 millimolar (mM) stock solution in DMSO, therefore dissolve 5 mg SB431542 in 260.5 µL DMSO. Make aliquots á 50 µL and store at -20 °C . Once thawed, keep at 4 °C . B27 supplement (50X) Thaw B27 supplement Overnight at 4 °C . Make aliquots in working volumes (e.g. 1 mL ) and store at -20 °C . Do not freeze-thaw more than twice. N-2 supplement (100X) Thaw N-2 supplement Overnight at 4 °C . Aliquot in working volumes (e.g. 0.5 mL) and store at -20 °C . Do not freeze-thaw more than twice. Neural Induction Supplement ( 10 mL ) Thaw Neural Induction Supplement overnight at 4 °C . Aliquot in working volumes (e.g. 1 mL ) and store at -20 °C . Do not freeze-thaw more than once.

steps.title: Day 7 Prepare a single cell...
steps.instruction: Day 7 Prepare a single cell suspension from one well NSCs using TrypLE Select or Accutase.

steps.title: Before starting prepare
steps.instruction: Before starting prepare: 1. Geltrex-coated vessels before performing the next steps. 2. 10 mL Neurobasal Medium containing 10 micromolar (µM) ROCK inhibitor and keep it at 37 °C until use. 3. 50 mL Neural Expansion Media (NEM) according to table 6. Table 6. Preparation of Neural Expansion Medium (NEM) %s %s

steps.title: Resuspend the NSCs in 1 mL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the NSCs in 1 mL Neurobasal media supplemented with ROCK inhibitor ( 10 micromolar (µM) ) and perform cell count.

steps.title: Decant the supernatant into a waste...
steps.instruction: Decant the supernatant into a waste bottle and carefully blot the tube on a cloth.

steps.title: Add 1 mL of FACS buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of FACS buffer (Table 2) to the cell suspension in the FACS tubes.

steps.title: Prepare three FACS tubes with 2...
steps.instruction: Prepare three FACS tubes with 2 - 3 x 10 5 cells per tube: Tube 1: non-stained sample (auto-fluorescence) Tube 2: SOX2-FITC/PAX6-APC Tube 3: SOX1-FITC/NESTIN-PE It is recommended to add to the FACS panel respective tubes for isotype control according to antibodies host, concentration and fluorophore. It is recommended for FACS panel establishment to prepare single stainings to perform compensation during or post-acquisition of fluorophores with overlapping emission spectra.

steps.title: Wash cells by adding 0
steps.instruction: Wash cells by adding 0.5 mL of cold permeabilisation buffer (Table 4)

steps.title: Decant the supernatant into a waste...
steps.instruction: Decant the supernatant into a waste bottle and carefully blot the tube on a cloth. Do not tip the tube to avoid losing cells.

steps.title: Centrifuge cells at 300 x g, , 3 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge cells at 300 x g, , 3 minutes .

steps.title: Mix well and incubate for 00:30:00...
steps.instruction: Mix well and incubate for 00:30:00 in the dark at 4 °C .

steps.title: Resuspend sample pellets in 0
steps.instruction: Resuspend sample pellets in 0.5 mL of cold, freshly prepared fixation solution (Table 3).

steps.title: Centrifuge at 300 x g, , 3 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 300 x g, , 3 minutes .

steps.title: Wash cells by adding 1 mL...
steps.instruction: Wash cells by adding 1 mL of cold permeabilization buffer (Table 4).

steps.title: Remove cryotube containing NSCs from liquid...
steps.instruction: Remove cryotube containing NSCs from liquid nitrogen and perform a quick thaw in a 37 °C water bath. Carefully swirl the cryotube in the water, avoid immersing it above the level of the cap. Remove the cryotube from the warm bath when still contains a remnant piece of ice.

steps.title: Centrifuge the cells at 300 x...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the cells at 300 x g, , 5 minutes at Room temperature .

steps.title: Resuspend each pellet in 100 µL of cold permeabilisation buffer
steps.instruction: Resuspend each pellet in 100 µL of cold permeabilisation buffer.

steps.title: Decant the supernatant into a waste...
steps.instruction: Decant the supernatant into a waste bottle and carefully blot the tube on a cloth.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 300 x g, , 3 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 300 x g, , 3 minutes .

steps.title: Centrifuge at 300 x g, , 3 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 300 x g, , 3 minutes .

steps.title: Wash cells by adding 0
steps.instruction: Wash cells by adding 0.5 mL of cold permeabilisation buffer.

steps.title: Mix well and incubate for 01:00:00...
steps.instruction: Mix well and incubate for 01:00:00 in the dark at 4 °C .

steps.title: Add antibodies to tubes 2 and...
steps.instruction: Add antibodies to tubes 2 and 3 according to Table 5. Table 5. Antibody staining %s %s

steps.title: Gently pipet the cell suspension up...
steps.instruction: Gently pipet the cell suspension up and down for 3-4 times using a 1000 µL pipette to break up any remaining cell clumps and detach residual cells from plate surface. If clumps are still seen, use a 1000 µL microliter pipette to pass the cell suspension through a 100 μm cell strainer and transfer them to a 50 mL conical tube.

steps.title: To stop the enzymatic reaction add...
steps.instruction: To stop the enzymatic reaction add 2 mL of pre-warmed NIM containing 10 micromolar (µM) ROCK inhibitor.

steps.title: Aspirate PBS and add 1 mL...
steps.instruction: Aspirate PBS and add 1 mL of TrypLE Select or Accutase to each well and incubate for 00:10:00 at 37 °C until most cells detach. After incubation, tap the culture vessel to detach all cells. Incubation time for enzymatic dissociation can be extended additional 00:05:00 to 00:10:00 when NSCs are tighly attached. Do not pipette the cells. Mechanical dissociation at this point will result in cell death. It requieres experience to be able to balance incubation times to accelerate the dissociation process without causing excesively cell death.

steps.title: After 24:00:00 place NSC bank in...
steps.instruction: After 24:00:00 place NSC bank in a liquid nitrogen tank for long-term storage.

steps.title: Remove supernatant and resuspend pellet in...
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant and resuspend pellet in an adequate volume of Bambanker containing 10 micromolar (µM) ROCK inhibitor. The cell concentration should be in the range of 1.5 – 3.0 x 10 6 NSCs per mL of Bambanker.

steps.title: Resuspend each pellet in 300 µL...
steps.instruction: Resuspend each pellet in 300 µL of FACS buffer and proceed to FACS measurement. It is recommended to mix stained samples gently. If cell clumps are still visible, pipet the cell suspensions through a 40 µm cell strainer into 5 mL FACS tubes. Perform FACS analysis immediately or store the samples closed and dark at 4°C up to 1 week. Double positive SOX2-FITC/PAX6-APC above 85-90% Double positive SOX1-FITC/NESTIN-PE above 85-90%

steps.title: When NSCs reach confluency (usually day...
steps.instruction: When NSCs reach confluency (usually day 7), wash cells with 1 mL of PBS without Ca 2+ and Mg 2+ per well of 6 well-plate. From one 100% confluent well of a 6-well plate, up to 1.0 – 1.5 x 10 7 cells can be harvested. It is recommended to freeze 1.5 – 3.0 x 10 6 NSCs per cryotube. Based on these estimations, prepare up to 10 cryotubes for one 6 well-plate. Make sure to have enough labelled tubes ready in advance, or use barcoded tubes.

steps.title: Seed 1 x 10 5 – 1
steps.instruction: Seed 1 x 10 5 – 1.5 x 10 5 NSCs/cm 2 in Geltrex coated vessels and incubate at 37 °C , 5% CO 2 incubator for 24:00:00 . Optimal NSC seeding density may vary between hiPSC lines. It is recommended to test 3-4 seeding densities close to the range suggested in this step. It is crucial to seed NSCs densely as they require good cell-to-cell contact.

steps.title: Gently pipet up and down to break up any clumps
steps.instruction: Gently pipet up and down to break up any clumps.

steps.title: Slowly (drop wise) add about 9...
steps.instruction: Slowly (drop wise) add about 9 mL of Neurobasal Medium containing 10 micromolar (µM) ROCK inhibitor to the cells, while gently swirling the tube. This step helps reducing osmotic shock.

steps.title: Using a 5 mL pipette remove...
steps.instruction: Using a 5 mL pipette remove cells from cryotube and transfer them to a 15 mL falcon tube.

steps.title: Disinfect the tube by wiping with...
steps.instruction: Disinfect the tube by wiping with 80% Ethanol using a tissue.

steps.title: Perform cell count using Trypan Blue and document cell viability
steps.instruction: Perform cell count using Trypan Blue and document cell viability. Viability at thawing above 70-80% is recommended as QC upon thawing.

steps.title: Remove the supernatant and resuspend pellet...
steps.instruction: Remove the supernatant and resuspend pellet in 2 mL of NEM containing 10 micromolar (µM) ROCK inhibitor.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 300 x g, , 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 300 x g, , 5 minutes .

steps.title: The day after, change media to...
steps.instruction: The day after, change media to NEM without ROCK inhibitor and monitor cell growth over the next days. As reference, use 2 mL media per well of a 6 well-plate. Volume should be adapted to culture density avoiding high metabolic environment.

steps.title: It is recommended to monitor cell...
steps.instruction: It is recommended to monitor cell morphology by taking microscopic pictures. See Figure 1 and 2 for exemplary cell morphology during the differentiation. Figure 1. Representative pictures showing morphology change of hiPSC line BIHi250-A (day -1, ROCKi morphology) until day 1 of differentiation. On day 0 (day of induction) the cells should be about 50-80% dense. During the next days, the cells become 100% dense. Figure 2. Representative images showing the morphology change of hiPSC line BIHi250-A between day 5 and 7 (endpoint) during neural induction and differentiation.

steps.title: Day 4-6 On day 4 of...
steps.instruction: Day 4-6 On day 4 of neural induction, cells will be reaching confluency. Increase volume of pre-warmed complete NIM per well to 5 mL .

steps.title: Perform cell count
steps.instruction: Perform cell count.

steps.title: Transfer 1 mL of cell suspension (containing 1
steps.instruction: Transfer 1 mL of cell suspension (containing 1.5 – 3.0 x 10 6 cells) per cryotube. Place the cryotubes in a freezing container and store at -80°C freezer Overnight . When available, the use of an automated controlled freezing device is recommended.

steps.title: Centrifuge NSC suspension at 300 x...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge NSC suspension at 300 x g, Room temperature, for 00:05:00

steps.title: Before starting prepare solutions needed for...
steps.instruction: Before starting prepare solutions needed for FACS staining as follows: A. Prepare a buffer containing PBS (pH 7.5), 0.5% bovine serum albumin (BSA) and 2 mM EDTA according to Table 2. Table 2. Preparation of FACS buffer %s %s B. Fixation and permeabilization buffer Prepared according to manufacturer´s instructions. For reference see Tables 3 and 4. FoxP3 Staining Buffer Set Miltenyi Biotec Catalog #130-093-142 It is recommended to freshly prepare the buffers and keep them at 4°C during the whole process. Table 3. Preparation of fixation buffer %s %s Table 4. Preparation of permeabilization buffer %s %s

steps.title: Optional QC on NSC culture upon...
steps.instruction: Optional QC on NSC culture upon expansion include marker staining (immunofluorescence or FACS) for SOX2, PAX6, NESTIN, SOX1. For FACS refer to steps 9-30.
